commands.repair_reason: - No automated repair is needed; maintainers should review and accept the upgrade-sensitive compatibility source-of-truth change before merge.; Cleared: The diff changes local TypeScript update-path logic and tests only; it adds no dependencies, workflows, secret handling, artifact downloads, or package metadata changes. commands.review_summary:
The PR passes the installed target OpenClaw version into post-core plugin convergence/update paths and adds regression tests for the stale 2026.5.28 to 2026.6.8 Feishu compatibility-host scenario.

PR surface: Source +1, Tests +55. Total +56 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible: current main can evaluate current-process post-core plugin compatibility against stale VERSION during upgrades, and discovery uses that host value for openclaw.compat.pluginApi checks. I did not run a Windows npm-global Feishu round trip in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - No automated repair is needed; maintainers should review and accept the upgrade-sensitive compatibility source-of-truth change before merge.

target.title: fix #93908: [Bug]: [Bug]: Feishu plugin skipping discovery - host reports 2026.5.28 despite 2026.6.8 install
target.body:
Fixes #93908

## Summary

- Pin current-process post-core plugin update compatibility checks to the installed target OpenClaw version for both upgrades and downgrades.
- Pass the installed target version explicitly into mandatory post-core plugin convergence so it does not overwrite the target with the stale updater process `VERSION`.
- Add regression coverage for the 2026.5.28 -> 2026.6.8 Feishu/plugin API compatibility path.

## What Problem This Solves

- Behavior or issue addressed: Feishu/Lark plugin discovery can be skipped after a package update when post-core plugin update logic still evaluates plugin API compatibility against the pre-update host version. The reported Feishu package declares `openclaw.compat.pluginApi: >=2026.6.8`, but a stale updater process can still report host `2026.5.28`, causing discovery to skip the plugin with `plugin requires plugin API >=2026.6.8, but this host is 2026.5.28; skipping discovery`.
- Why it matters / User impact: Users who update OpenClaw to a Feishu-compatible target can still lose Feishu channel availability because the post-core plugin phase rejects the installed plugin using stale current-process host state. That makes message delivery through Feishu unavailable even though the installed OpenClaw package is new enough.
- What did NOT change: This does not change Feishu package metadata, plugin API ranges, semver compatibility enforcement, plugin manifest parsing, default install choices, or runtime fallback behavior for genuinely incompatible plugins.
- Architecture / source-of-truth check: The public contract surface remains `openclaw.compat.pluginApi` and the existing compatibility resolver. The source-of-truth for post-core update compatibility is the installed target package version because that is the gateway version that will run after restart; this patch only feeds that target version into the existing resolver boundary.
- Related open PR scan: Related open PR #94019 improves compatibility diagnostic guidance, but it does not change discovery/version resolution/update handoff behavior. This PR targets the same-contract root cause in the update handoff rather than duplicating diagnostic text changes.

## Root Cause

- Root cause: The current-process post-core update path could still be running code whose module `VERSION` is the pre-update version (`2026.5.28`) while the installed package root had already been updated to `2026.6.8`. Fresh post-core child processes already receive `OPENCLAW_COMPATIBILITY_HOST_VERSION=<installed target>`, but the current-process path only pinned that override for downgrades. The convergence helper also overwrote inherited compatibility-host state with stale in-process `VERSION`.
- Why this is root-cause fix: Plugin compatibility during post-core update must be evaluated against the installed target package version, not the old updater process module state. Pinning `OPENCLAW_COMPATIBILITY_HOST_VERSION` to the installed target for the current-process plugin phase and passing the same target into convergence restores that lifecycle invariant, so the existing discovery gate makes the same decision the restarted gateway will make instead of masking the symptom with a Feishu-specific exception.

## Why This Change Was Made

The installed target package version is the canonical source of truth for post-core plugin compatibility. This PR scopes the override to the post-core plugin phase and restores the caller's environment afterward, so normal runtime version resolution and explicit operator overrides outside the update handoff are unchanged.

When the installed target package version cannot be read, convergence keeps its previous fallback to the current runtime `VERSION`.

## Verification

- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/update-cli/post-core-plugin-convergence.test.ts src/cli/update-cli.test.ts --reporter=dot --runInBand` exited 0. `pnpm format:fix src/cli/update-cli/post-core-plugin-convergence.ts src/cli/update-cli/post-core-plugin-convergence.test.ts src/cli/update-cli/update-command.ts src/cli/update-cli.test.ts` exited 0; local Node printed the existing engine warning that the repo wants `>=22.19.0` while this machine has `v22.17.0`.
- Target test file: `src/cli/update-cli/post-core-plugin-convergence.test.ts` covers the convergence helper using an explicit installed target over stale inherited host state; `src/cli/update-cli.test.ts` covers the current-process 2026.5.28 -> 2026.6.8 upgrade path pinning the environment during post-core plugin update and restoring it afterward.
- Scenario locked in: The regression tests lock in that a package update from host `2026.5.28` to installed target `2026.6.8` passes `2026.6.8` to plugin convergence and exposes `OPENCLAW_COMPATIBILITY_HOST_VERSION=2026.6.8` while `updateNpmInstalledPlugins` runs, even though the updater process itself is still old.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: The focused tests exercise the update/convergence lifecycle boundary that owns the stale-version handoff, while the Feishu discovery proof exercises the real plugin API decision. No broad end-to-end network fixture is needed to prove that discovery no longer uses the stale host version.

## Real behavior proof

- Real environment tested: Windows local worktree, current OpenClaw source, real Feishu package metadata from this repo (`@openclaw/feishu` version `2026.6.8`, `openclaw.compat.pluginApi: >=2026.6.8`).
- Evidence after fix: With stale update-process state (`OPENCLAW_VERSION=2026.5.28`) and compatibility host pinned to `2026.5.28`, Feishu was not discovered and discovery emitted `plugin requires plugin API >=2026.6.8, but this host is 2026.5.28; skipping discovery`. With the same stale `OPENCLAW_VERSION=2026.5.28` but compatibility host pinned to the installed target `2026.6.8`, Feishu was discovered and no plugin API skip diagnostic was emitted.
- Observed result after fix: The decision boundary from #93908 changes from “stale host rejects Feishu” to “installed target host discovers Feishu”; this confirms the post-core compatibility host version is the variable that controls the reported skip.
- What was not tested: A Feishu network/message round trip was not completed. A temporary Feishu app credential was available, but this local environment could not complete the external `open.feishu.cn` fetch; the submitted proof covers the Windows/local discovery and update-version behavior that caused the skip.

## Regression Test Plan

- Fix classification: Root-cause fix for the update/plugin convergence version handoff; it changes the source-of-truth handoff used by existing compatibility checks rather than adding a Feishu-specific exception.
- Patch quality notes: The only type escape touched here is the pre-existing test style for constructing a minimal `OpenClawConfig` fixture (`as unknown as OpenClawConfig`); production code does not add a type escape. Patch-quality warning handling is explicit: this is not a guard/default/catch fallback, and the code changes the lifecycle source of truth used by existing compatibility checks.
- Maintainer-ready confidence: High for the stale-version handoff and discovery decision boundary because the production path has focused regression tests plus real Feishu metadata discovery proof. The only lower-confidence area is external Feishu network delivery, which is disclosed as not tested.

## Merge risk

- Risk labels considered: `merge-risk:availability`, `merge-risk:compatibility`, `merge-risk:update`, `merge-risk:plugin-runtime`.
- Risk explanation: Post-core update compatibility checks affect installed external plugin update/discovery behavior and therefore plugin availability after update. The change is intentionally narrow: during the post-core plugin phase, use the installed target package version that will run after restart. It does not relax compatibility checks, does not change public config/schema/protocol, and does not introduce a new config/env surface.
- Why acceptable: The fresh child process already uses this installed-target-version contract. This PR makes the current-process path and convergence helper follow the same contract, restores the previous environment after the scoped phase, and preserves the existing runtime `VERSION` fallback if the target version cannot be read.

## Non-goals

- Do not change Feishu package metadata or plugin API requirements.
- Do not relax or bypass plugin API compatibility checks.
- Do not add runtime fallbacks for incompatible plugins.
target.author: mikasa0818
